---
title: "Environment"
format: html
---

### Target Environment

Concert Operate is deployed on a self-hosted **OpenShift Container Platform (OCP)** cluster.
The cluster is dedicated to the Concert Operate workload and provisioned with the following
general characteristics:

- **Platform:** OpenShift Container Platform (OCP), version confirmed at deployment time
- **Deployment model:** Dedicated OCP cluster; Concert Operate deployed into a dedicated namespace
- **Container registry:** Internal Artifactory instance acting as a pull-through proxy for
  `icr.io` and `cp.icr.io`. IBM entitlement key is not required when using this proxy path.
- **Storage:** Nutanix CSI storage provisioner; `xfs` filesystem; storage class confirmed at
  deployment time
- **Image availability:** All required Concert Operate images available via the Artifactory
  proxy: `icr.io/cpopen`, `icr.io/cpopen/cpfs`, `cp.icr.io/cp`, `cp.icr.io/cp/cp4waiops`
  paths all validated

### Event Ingestion Path

Events originate from the batch scheduler's operations management layer. The near-term
ingestion path uses an existing event forwarding agent (`postemsg`-style) on the source
system. The agent is retargeted to deliver events to Concert Operate rather than the
legacy event aggregator. No changes to the batch scheduler itself are required.

**Connectivity:** The event source system must have outbound network access to the Concert
Operate ingestion endpoint on `<CONCERT_OPERATE_HOSTNAME>:<PORT>`. Firewall rules and
network security requests should be scoped to this single destination.

### Integration Target

Correlated incidents are forwarded to **ServiceNow Event Management** as the ITSM target.
This aligns with the client environment's transition from a legacy event aggregator to
ServiceNow Event Management as the primary event consolidation layer.
